headline: Ethiopian 787 Order Underscores Boeing Widebody Demand And Valuation Case
summary: Ethiopian Airlines has fully exercised an order for six additional Boeing 787 Dreamliner jets. The deal expands the carrier's 787-9 fleet to support long haul passenger and cargo demand. This move builds on a previously announced widebody order and reinforces confidence in Boeing's aircraft programs. For investors watching Boeing (NYSE:BA), this fresh 787 commitment comes as widebody aircraft remain central to long haul travel and cargo routes. African aviation markets are growing quickly,...

content.title: Ethiopian 787 Order Underscores Boeing Widebody Demand And Valuation Case
content.summary: Ethiopian Airlines has fully exercised an order for six additional Boeing 787 Dreamliner jets. The deal expands the carrier's 787-9 fleet to support long haul passenger and cargo demand. This move builds on a previously announced widebody order and reinforces confidence in Boeing's aircraft programs. For investors watching Boeing (NYSE:BA), this fresh 787 commitment comes as widebody aircraft remain central to long haul travel and cargo routes. African aviation markets are growing quickly,...

headline: Boeing’s (NYSE:BA) Q1 CY2026 Sales Top Estimates
summary: Aerospace and defense company Boeing (NYSE:BA) reported Q1 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 14% year on year to $22.22 billion. Its non-GAAP loss of $0.20 per share was 70.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Trump Cease-Fire Extension Lifts Stocks. Why Markets Are Still on Edge.
summary: U.S. stocks are set to advance through another extended deadline on peace talks with Iran, with markets poised for solid gains following their first two-day decline in three weeks.  President Donald Trump effectively issued an indefinite suspension of his Wednesday deadline for talks with Iran after the close of trading on Tuesday, declaring that the U.S. will hold off on attacking the nation until a new proposal is put forward, or “discussions are concluded, one way or the other.”  “Based on the fact that the Government of Iran is seriously fractured, not unexpectedly so, we have been asked to hold our Attack on the Country of Iran until such time as their leaders and representatives can come up with a unified proposal,” Trump said.

content.title: Trump Cease-Fire Extension Lifts Stocks. Why Markets Are Still on Edge.
content.summary: U.S. stocks are set to advance through another extended deadline on peace talks with Iran, with markets poised for solid gains following their first two-day decline in three weeks.  President Donald Trump effectively issued an indefinite suspension of his Wednesday deadline for talks with Iran after the close of trading on Tuesday, declaring that the U.S. will hold off on attacking the nation until a new proposal is put forward, or “discussions are concluded, one way or the other.”  “Based on the fact that the Government of Iran is seriously fractured, not unexpectedly so, we have been asked to hold our Attack on the Country of Iran until such time as their leaders and representatives can come up with a unified proposal,” Trump said.

headline: US stocks climb, but so do oil prices with uncertainty rising about the war with Iran
summary: GE Vernova, Boston Scientific and Boeing are helping to lead the U.S. stock market toward another record Wednesday after joining the list of companies reporting fatter profits for the start of the year than analysts expected.  The S&P 500 climbed 0.6% and was on track to top its all-time high set on Friday.  GE Vernova flew 12.1% higher after the company, whose products help generate about a quarter of the world’s electricity, reported profit for the first three months of the year that blew past analysts’ expectations.

content.title: US stocks climb, but so do oil prices with uncertainty rising about the war with Iran
content.summary: GE Vernova, Boston Scientific and Boeing are helping to lead the U.S. stock market toward another record Wednesday after joining the list of companies reporting fatter profits for the start of the year than analysts expected.  The S&P 500 climbed 0.6% and was on track to top its all-time high set on Friday.  GE Vernova flew 12.1% higher after the company, whose products help generate about a quarter of the world’s electricity, reported profit for the first three months of the year that blew past analysts’ expectations.

headline: Boeing reports narrowing loss, points to progress on turnaround
summary: Boeing reported a small quarterly loss Wednesday following an uptick in commercial plane deliveries as the aerospace giant pointed to progress on a turnaround after a long period of stumbles.Boeing's first-quarter 2026 results included an uptick in commercial plane deliveries as well as revenue growth in global services and defense, space & security, where Boeing secured a new agreements with the US Pentagon.
